task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)

title.b: Impossible to print jobs with a matte print mode

section.title: Symptom
section.p: A job using a matte print mode is submitted to the Colorado printer but does not appear on the operator panel.

section.title: Possible cause
section.p: Some printers, like the Colorado 1630, require a license for matte print modes.

Without such a license jobs using a matte print mode will be deleted by the printer. They will not appear on the operator panel. There will be an error message logged in the datalog.xml like "Incorrect job detected: PrintMode Matte_20 is NOT supported" with error code 11956.

section.title: Tests/Checks
section.p: Check the license for matte print modes in the Settings Editor (Support > Licenses).

note.p: In ONYX and Caldera it is possible to configure whether the printer supports matte print modes or not. Other RIPs supporting the Colorado 1630 might support this as well. If matte print modes are disabled in the RIP, then you will get an error message when you want to print a job with a matte print mode.

title.i: Configure matte print modes seetting in ONYX / Caldera

li.p: Open "Configure Printer" and select "Features" in the "Device" tab:
Select "Global Printer Options" and click on "Configure":
To disable matte print modes: uncheck the tick box for "Printing with matte print modes":
Go to "Server admin", select the printer and next "Configure". On the "Connection" tab the setting "Print with matte print modes" can be set to "enabled" or "disabled".
